title: cracca pauciflora
intro: Cracca pauciflora, also known as the few-flowered broom, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, reducing fever, and relieving digestive problems. The herbal preparations of Cracca pauciflora typically involve drying and infusing the roots and leaves to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
